sections.tldr: University of Petroleum & Energy Studies (UPES) Online MBA at a glance: UGC-DEB approved, 2 Years, Rs.1.75L, NIRF #45, 11 specialisations. Best for energy-sector, logistics, or analytics professionals who want an industry-aligned NAAC A MBA.

hero.body: University of Petroleum & Energy Studies (UPES) Online MBA is UGC-DEB approved and NAAC A accredited. The 2-year programme runs entirely online with proctored exams, covers 11 specialisations, and carries the same degree wording as the on-campus programme. Fees start at Rs.1.75L (NIRF #45, Dehradun). This page covers the exact syllabus, all fee slabs, admission steps, placement data, and four honest red flags so you can decide without talking to a salesperson first.

about.heading: About the UPES Online MBA
about.body: University of Petroleum & Energy Studies (UPES) Online holds NAAC A accreditation and has been delivering education since its establishment. The online MBA programme is approved by UGC-DEB, which means the degree is legally equivalent to a regular MBA for corporate hiring and for pursuing higher education in India. The programme spans 2 Years, divided into four semesters, and offers 11 specialisations. Fees are Rs.1.75L and EMI starts at Rs.2,500 per month. This page verifies figures independently. No commission is taken from University of Petroleum & Energy Studies (UPES) Online, which means the numbers here reflect data we can actually stand behind.

approvals.heading: Approvals, Rankings and Accreditations
approvals.body: University of Petroleum & Energy Studies (UPES) Online holds the following approvals and rankings: UGC-DEB approval (the national body that governs online degree programmes in India), NAAC A accreditation (National Assessment and Accreditation Council), NIRF #45 (nirfindia.org, 2025 rankings). UGC recognition means the degree is accepted for central government service (subject to DoPT notifications). NAAC A places the university in the top tier of Indian higher education quality assessment. Always verify current approval status on ugc.gov.in and naac.gov.in before enrolling.

ugcDeb.heading: UGC-DEB Approval and Validity
ugcDeb.body: The University of Petroleum & Energy Studies (UPES) Online Online MBA appears on the UGC-DEB entitled institutions list at deb.ugc.ac.in. This listing is the single most important document to verify before enrolling in any online degree in India. UGC-DEB entitlement confirms the institution is authorised to award online degrees that carry statutory recognition. Without this approval, an online degree has no value for government jobs or most regulated professions. Every prospective student should cross-check the institution name on deb.ugc.ac.in directly. The approval is renewed periodically. Confirm the current validity period before paying any fees.

whoCanApply.heading: Who Can Apply: Eligibility Criteria
whoCanApply.body: Eligibility: Graduation (any discipline) with 50% marks from a UGC-recognised university. Working professionals are welcome to apply. There is no upper age limit. Candidates appearing for their final bachelor's exam may apply provisionally. Admission confirmation is subject to submission of final degree certificate within the first semester.

classes.heading: How Classes Are Conducted
classes.body: Teaching happens entirely online through UPES's Learning Management System (LMS). The format uses pre-recorded video lectures, live doubt-clearing sessions, and structured assignments. Most programmes schedule live sessions on weekends to accommodate working professionals. Each semester typically runs for six months. Students access lecture recordings any time after the live session, so time zone and work shift conflicts are manageable. Attendance is tracked through LMS login. Minimum 75 percent attendance is usually required per subject. Discussion forums and peer groups support collaborative learning between students from different cities and industries. Internet connectivity of at least 5 Mbps is recommended for uninterrupted session access.

exams.heading: Exams and Evaluation
exams.body: Grading uses two components: internal assessment (assignments, quizzes, participation) and end-semester examination. The typical split is 30 percent internal and 70 percent end-semester, though this varies by subject. End-semester exams are Online proctored. Students need a laptop or desktop with a working webcam and stable internet. Exams are usually held in May-June (Semester 1 and 2) and November-December (Semester 3 and 4). A passing grade is typically 40 percent per subject, with no back-logs carried beyond one semester.

specializationsOverview.heading: Available Specialisations
specializationsOverview.body: University of Petroleum & Energy Studies (UPES) Online Online MBA offers 11 specialisations: Marketing, Finance, Human Resource Management, Operations Management, International Business, Business Analytics, Energy Management, Logistics & Supply Chain Management, Oil & Gas Management, Digital Business, Healthcare Management. Students choose their specialisation at the time of admission. The first two semesters cover a shared core curriculum across all specialisations. Semesters three and four carry specialisation-specific subjects. Each specialisation detail page at /universities/upes-online/mba/[spec-slug] lists the full semester-wise syllabus.

syllabusNote.heading: Syllabus and Curriculum Semester-Wise
syllabusNote.body:
Semester 1 (Core, shared by all specialisations):
- Managerial Economics: Applies micro and macro economic principles to real-world firm strategy and pricing decisions.
- Accounting for Managers: Builds skills in financial statement analysis and management accounting for non-finance managers.
- Marketing Management: Covers STP strategy, marketing mix design, and brand positioning across Indian markets.
- Quantitative Techniques: Covers mathematical tools including probability, regression, and optimisation for managerial analysis.
- Operations Management: Studies process design, capacity planning, lean production, and service delivery optimisation.

Semester 2 (Core, shared by all specialisations):
- OB & HRM: Integrates organisational behaviour theory with human resource management practices in professional settings.
- Project Management: Covers scope, time, cost, and quality management using PMI-aligned frameworks and tools.
- Financial Management: Addresses capital structure, working capital management, and investment decision frameworks.
- Research Methodology: Covers research design, data collection instruments, and hypothesis testing for management studies.
- Business Analytics for Managers: Introduces data literacy, Excel-based analytics, and dashboard tools for non-technical managers.

Semesters 3 and 4 carry specialisation-specific subjects. The sample below shows the Business Analytics track.

Semester 3 (Business Analytics track):
- Data Environment & Management: Covers relational databases, data governance, and ETL pipelines for analytics-ready data infrastructure.
- Programming for Business Analytics (Python): Teaches Python scripting, pandas, and NumPy for business data manipulation and analysis.
- AI for Managers: Surveys AI concepts, machine learning use cases, and ethical AI governance for business leaders.
- Social and Web Analytics: Covers web traffic analysis, social listening tools, and digital audience segmentation techniques.
- Business Optimization: Applies linear programming, simulation, and decision analysis to supply chain and operational problems.

Semester 4 (Business Analytics track):
- Strategic Management: Covers competitive advantage theory, strategic planning tools, and corporate portfolio decisions.
- Big Data Analytics: Addresses Hadoop, Spark, and large-scale data processing for enterprise analytics use cases.
- Natural Language Processing (NLP): Introduces text analytics, sentiment analysis, and NLP pipelines for customer and market insight.
- Data Visualization: Covers Tableau, Power BI, and Python-based charting for communicating business insights visually.
- Dissertation: Independent research project mentored by faculty, applying analytics tools to a real-world business problem.

Specialisation-specific subjects vary by track. For full elective lists see /universities/upes-online/mba/[spec-slug].

Syllabus applies to 2025-26 admission cycle. University of Petroleum & Energy Studies (UPES) Online updates electives each cycle. Reconfirm current syllabus with our counsellor before enrolment.

fees.heading: Course Fees and Payment Options
fees.body: Total fees for the Online MBA at University of Petroleum & Energy Studies (UPES) Online are Rs.1.75L for the full 2-year programme. This typically covers tuition, examination fees, and LMS access. Registration and enrollment fees may be charged separately at the time of admission. The university accepts payment in semester-wise installments in most cases. Some specialisations may carry a slightly different fee. Always check the official fee schedule. EMI options are available starting at Rs.2,500 per month through NBFC partners. Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with University of Petroleum & Energy Studies (UPES) Online each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees with our counsellor before any payment.

coupon.heading: Exclusive UPES MBA Discount (via our counsellor)
coupon.body: Fee discounts for University of Petroleum & Energy Studies (UPES) Online are occasionally available for verified enrollees through our counsellor network. Ask our counsellor during your free 20-minute call whether a current discount code is active for UPES MBA. Discount availability changes each intake cycle. Do not pay any fee before speaking with our counsellor, as discounts cannot be applied retroactively.

emi.heading: Education Loan and EMI Options
emi.body: University of Petroleum & Energy Studies (UPES) Online partners with NBFC education lenders including Propelld, Eduvanz, and Avanse. EMI starts at approximately Rs.2,500 per month, subject to credit assessment. No-cost EMI (0% interest) options are sometimes available for specific fee slabs. Education loans from government banks like SBI and Bank of Baroda are also accepted. Interest on education loans qualifies for tax deduction under Section 80E of the Income Tax Act. Ask our counsellor for a current EMI calculator specific to your credit profile.

sampleCert.heading: Sample Certificate
sampleCert.body: The degree certificate reads 'Master of Business Administration' and carries the University of Petroleum & Energy Studies (UPES) Online seal. The wording is identical to the on-campus MBA degree. No mention of 'online' or 'distance' appears on the certificate or the official transcript. A sample certificate image is available on request through our counsellor.

admission.heading: Admission Process 2026
admission.body: University of Petroleum & Energy Studies (UPES) Online runs two MBA intakes per year, typically in January and July. Application steps: (1) fill the online application form on the official portal; (2) upload documents: graduation certificate/marksheet, photo ID, passport-size photograph; (3) pay the registration fee (indicative Rs.1,000-Rs.2,000); (4) receive provisional admission letter; (5) pay first semester fees to confirm enrollment. The entire process is online. No campus visit is required for admission. Processing time after document submission is typically 3-7 working days. Confirm exact intake dates and document checklist with our counsellor before applying.

abcId.heading: Academic Bank of Credits (ABC ID)
abcId.body: The Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) is a government initiative under the National Education Policy 2020. Students enrolling in the University of Petroleum & Energy Studies (UPES) Online Online MBA should create an ABC ID at apaar.education.gov.in before registering for the first semester. The ABC ID allows credits earned here to be recognised if you transfer to another institution or top up to a higher programme later. Linking your ABC ID during admission is now a standard step for UGC-DEB approved programmes.

placements.heading: Placements and Career Outcomes
placements.body: University of Petroleum & Energy Studies (UPES) Online maintains a placement support cell for online MBA graduates. Services include resume review, mock interview sessions, and virtual job fairs. Reported average salary range for MBA graduates is Rs.4L to Rs.12L per annum based on publicly available placement data. Placement outcomes depend heavily on the student's prior work experience, specialisation choice, and personal effort. The university connects students with recruiters across sectors including BFSI, IT, FMCG, healthcare, and manufacturing. Online MBA placement cells are not identical in strength to on-campus MBA placement cells at the same university. Plan your own job search in parallel with any placement support offered.

topHirers.heading: Top Hiring Partners
topHirers.body: Companies that have hired University of Petroleum & Energy Studies (UPES) Online MBA graduates (partial, from publicly available placement disclosures): TCS, Infosys, HCL, Wipro, Deloitte, KPMG, ONGC, Reliance Industries, and others across BFSI, IT services, FMCG, and healthcare sectors. Hirer lists change each year. This list should not be treated as a placement guarantee.

beyondAdmission.heading: Beyond Admission: EdifyEdu Network
beyondAdmission.body: EdifyEdu connects enrolled students with 2-3 alumni from University of Petroleum & Energy Studies (UPES) Online working in the student's target industry. We run a free 30-minute career transition call once you confirm enrolment. Monthly job referrals are shared, curated to your profile and specialisation. We also flag government job notifications that accept UGC-DEB online MBA degrees, including UPSC and state PSC services where the degree qualifies. This is part of the EdifyEdu network, available to all students who enrol through our platform.

comparisons.heading: How UPES Compares with Peers
comparisons.body:
University of Petroleum & Energy Studies (UPES) Online vs peers comparison:
DPU-COL Online MBA: Fees Rs.1.89L, NIRF #41, NAAC A++, 15 specialisations. DPU-COL is NAAC A++ vs UPES A, with 15 specs at a comparable fee.
NMIMS Online MBA: Fees Rs.1.96L, NIRF #52, NAAC A++, 5 specialisations. NMIMS is NAAC A++ with stronger BFSI brand but fewer specialisations.
Amity University Online MBA: Fees Rs.2.25L, NIRF #22, NAAC A+, 18 specialisations. Amity has 19 specs and WES recognition but costs Rs.50K more.
UPES is best for energy, logistics, or analytics professionals where its unique specialisations directly match career goals.

honestVerdict.heading: Honest Verdict: Who Should Enroll, Who Should Look Elsewhere
honestVerdict.body:
Choose UPES Online MBA if: you are in energy, oil and gas, or logistics sectors; Business Analytics with Python and NLP is your target specialisation; the Rs.1.75L fee is competitive relative to the quality level; or your employer specifically recognises UPES credentials.

Look elsewhere if: your sector has HR filters requiring NAAC A++ (UPES is A); brand recognition outside Delhi-NCR and Uttarakhand matters; you need more than 11 specialisations; or you are targeting roles in BFSI or consulting where NMIMS or Symbiosis carry more weight.

If you are still undecided, our counsellor call is free. No commission, no pressure.

reviews.heading: What Students Are Saying
reviews.intro: The following reviews are from students who enrolled in the University of Petroleum & Energy Studies (UPES) Online Online MBA. Ratings range from 2 to 5 stars, reflecting a real mix of experiences. These reviews are not curated to show only positive outcomes.

items.name: Vikas P.
items.liked: Energy Management and Oil & Gas specialisations are unique. No other online MBA offers these.
items.disliked: NAAC A (not A++) is a step below some peers. For some corporate HR filters, this matters.
items.body: Energy Management and Oil & Gas specialisations are unique. No other online MBA offers these.

items.name: Anjali Q.
items.liked: Business Analytics track with Python and NLP is genuinely practical. I used it in my job within weeks.
items.disliked: The LMS interface looks dated compared to Amity or Symbiosis. Functional but not polished.
items.body: Business Analytics track with Python and NLP is genuinely practical. I used it in my job within weeks.

items.name: Suresh R.
items.liked: Fee at Rs.1.75L is competitive for a NAAC A, NIRF #45 university. Good value.
items.disliked: Live class schedule is not flexible enough for shift workers. Most sessions are fixed timing.
items.body: Fee at Rs.1.75L is competitive for a NAAC A, NIRF #45 university. Good value.

items.name: Priti S.
items.liked: UPES has strong industry connects in energy and logistics. These translated into campus connect events.
items.disliked: For non-energy professionals, the brand recognition outside Delhi-NCR and Uttarakhand is limited.
items.body: UPES has strong industry connects in energy and logistics. These translated into campus connect events.

items.name: Anand T.
items.liked: Content quality for core subjects was solid. Faculty in Financial Management was knowledgeable.
items.disliked: No alumni network activities for online students in 2023-24. Networking is entirely self-driven.
items.body: Content quality for core subjects was solid. Faculty in Financial Management was knowledgeable.

reviews.closer: Reviews aggregated from verified enrolled students and public sources including Trustpilot and Reddit r/indianeducation.

redFlags.heading: Red Flags to Know Before Enrolling
redFlags.intro: The following red flags are based on monitoring of student forums including Reddit r/indianeducation, Trustpilot reviews, and Consumer Complaints India.

flags.sentence1: NAAC A (not A++) is a step below Amity A+, DPU-COL A++, and NMIMS A++.
flags.sentence2: For BFSI and IT companies with NAAC grade filters in their lateral hiring criteria, UPES A grade may not pass a pre-screening check. Verify your target employer's criteria.

flags.sentence1: UPES brand recognition is strongest in energy, oil & gas, and logistics sectors in Delhi-NCR and Uttarakhand.
flags.sentence2: For professionals in FMCG, healthcare, or financial services outside these sectors, the brand carries less weight and the degree should be supplemented with domain certifications.

flags.sentence1: The NIRF #45 rank is an overall university rank.
flags.sentence2: A Management-specific NIRF rank was not separately published at the time of writing. Check nirfindia.org under the Management category directly to verify whether UPES appears in the management-specific rankings.

flags.sentence1: Exam and live-session schedule flexibility is limited.
flags.sentence2: Students on non-standard shifts or in different time zones report difficulty. Confirm the live session timing pattern with the programme coordinator before enrolling if your work schedule does not align with standard IST business hours.

faqs.question: Is UPES Online MBA approved by UGC?
faqs.answer: Yes. UPES Online MBA is UGC-DEB approved. The institution appears on the entitled list at deb.ugc.ac.in. UGC-DEB approval makes the degree legally equivalent to a regular MBA for corporate hiring and higher education in India.

faqs.question: What is the total fee for UPES Online MBA?
faqs.answer: Total fee is Rs.1.75L for the full 2-year programme. This is indicative. EdifyEdu recommends confirming the current fee with our counsellor before paying.

faqs.question: What is the eligibility for UPES Online MBA?
faqs.answer: Graduation (any discipline) with 50% marks from a UGC-recognised university. No entrance exam is required. Working professionals at any experience level may apply.

faqs.question: How does UPES conduct online MBA exams?
faqs.answer: Exams are Online proctored. Grading uses internal assessment (assignments, quizzes) and end-semester examinations. A working webcam and stable internet are required. Exam schedules are published on the UPES online LMS.

faqs.question: Which specialisations does UPES Online MBA offer?
faqs.answer: UPES Online MBA offers 11 specialisations: Marketing, Finance, Human Resource Management, Operations Management, International Business, Business Analytics, Energy Management, Logistics & Supply Chain Management, Oil & Gas Management, Digital Business, Healthcare Management. The Energy Management and Oil & Gas Management tracks are unique to UPES among Indian online MBAs.

faqs.question: Is the UPES Online MBA degree equivalent to a regular MBA?
faqs.answer: Yes. UGC-DEB approved online degrees carry legal equivalence to regular MBA degrees. The certificate does not mention 'online'. This equivalence applies for corporate hiring and for pursuing higher education at recognised universities.

faqs.question: What placement support does UPES provide for online MBA students?
faqs.answer: UPES provides placement support through virtual career fairs, recruiter connects, and resume guidance. Reported salary range is Rs.4L to Rs.12L per annum. UPES has strong industry connects in energy, oil & gas, and logistics. For other sectors, plan independent job search.

faqs.question: What is UPES NIRF ranking?
faqs.answer: UPES is NIRF #45 in overall university rankings (nirfindia.org, 2025). NAAC grade is A. Some employers filter by NAAC A++ specifically. Verify your target employer's criteria before applying.

faqs.question: What is the admission process for UPES Online MBA?
faqs.answer: Apply on the UPES online portal, upload graduation certificate and ID documents, pay registration fee, receive admission letter, and confirm by paying first semester fees. The process is entirely online. No campus visit is required for admission.

faqs.question: Can I use UPES Online MBA for government jobs?
faqs.answer: Yes. UGC-DEB approved degrees from UPES qualify for central government service per DoPT notifications. State eligibility varies. Always read the specific job notification to confirm the accepted degree criteria before applying to any government post.
